title: Daucus leptophyllus
taxonomy: Daucus leptophyllus is classified as a species of plant within the genus Daucus, which belongs to the family Apiaceae. It is a type of wild carrot native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. Within the family Apiaceae, Daucus leptophyllus is a member of the subfamily Apioideae. The genus Daucus is closely related to other wild carrots and related species within the same family. This classification provides insight into the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Daucus leptophyllus.

intro: Daucus leptophyllus, commonly known as linear-leaf wild carrot or Lesser Carrot, is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Daucus leptophyllus include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
